title: Welcome to Docker
description: Welcome! In this first level, you'll learn how to start your very first container. Docker containers are isolated environments that run applications.
objective: Start a container named 'ubuntu' using the command: `docker run ubuntu`

title: Listing Containers
description: Great job! Now let's see which containers are running. The `docker ps` command shows you all active containers.

title: Networks: Connect Containers
description: Networks let containers talk to each other. We'll create a network and connect your Ubuntu container to it.
objective: Create a network `mynet` and connect the running `ubuntu` container to it.

title: Volumes: Mount Data
description: Volumes persist container data beyond the container lifecycle. We'll create a volume and mount it inside a container.
objective: Create/mount a volume `myvol` to `/data` inside an `ubuntu` container.

title: Compose: Multi-Container Setup
description: Docker Compose can orchestrate multiple containers together. This simulator will start a small multi-service topology.

title: Build: Image Layers
description: A Docker image is built from layers. We'll simulate building an image and visualizing its layer stack.
objective: Build an image named `myapp` with: `docker build -t myapp .`
